chunks.text: Course: EE 121 | EECS at UC Berkeley EE 121. Introduction to Digital Communication Systems Catalog Description: Introduction to the basic principles of the design and analysis of modern digital communication systems. Topics include source coding, channel coding, baseband and passband modulation techniques, receiver design, and channel equalization. Applications to design of digital telephone modems, compact disks, and digital wireless communication systems. Concepts illustrated by a sequence of MATLAB exercises. Units: 4 Also Offered As: ELENG 121 Related Areas: Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS) Prerequisites: EECS 16A, EECS 16B, and COMPSCI 70. Formats: Fall: 3 hours of lecture and 1 hour of discussion per week Spring: 3 hours of lecture and 1 hour of discussion per week Grading Basis: Student Option Final Exam Status: Yes Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
